entries.title: What We Talk About When We Talk About the Interstitium
entries.summary: “The interstitium seems plotless, an entity that, as Auden claimed of poetry, ‘makes nothing happen.’ ”

entries.title: How Could I Forget Odysseus?
entries.summary: “I loved that Odysseus could transition so easily from being somebody to being nobody, and back again."

entries.title: Charles Brockden Brown Declares Independence
entries.summary: Frequently claimed to be the first novel published in the United States, the book begins with a religious iconoclast who spontaneously combusts while worshiping in a temple.

entries.title: In Paris, in Tokyo, in Singapore, in Prague, I Stumble Out, Insane with Coffee
entries.summary: “In the pen barrel was a picture of a ferryboat, which sailed to the other end if you tilted the pen, turning the Mississippi into Niagara Falls.”
